executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 102 organizations compromised in a coordinated 48-hour attack wave on May 6, 2026. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (consumer goods), National Money Mart (financial services with database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board (education), Peterborough Public Health, and the Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware gang leads with 21 victims, followed by Play (11), Safepay (10), and Clop (10), targeting critical infrastructure including healthcare, education, public sector, and financial services. This represents a national-scale incident requiring immediate coordinated response.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 21 Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Canada Goose supplier Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Eastern Townships School Board, BioPharma Services, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), Workers Health & Safety Centre
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), Double extortion with data leak sites

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a coordinated ransomware crisis with 102 organizations breached on May 6, 2026, spanning all provinces and critical sectors. Public sector targets include Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé, Eastern Townships School Board, and Workers Health & Safety Centre. Financial services (National Money Mart with confirmed data leak, Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash) and healthcare (Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, bisonfamilymedical.com) sectors are heavily impacted. No CCCS advisories were identified in the reporting period.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (luxury apparel), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Kids & Company (childcare services) compromised. No incidents identified at Loblaws, Canadian Tire, SportChek, or Shoppers Drug Mart.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company confirmed database leak via Everest ransomware gang. Morison Insurance Brokers and Trucash also compromised. Multiple accounting/financial firms breached including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, reidhurstnagy.com, and Axion50plus. No incidents identified at major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO).

report_2w.executive_summary: CVE-2026-31431, a Linux vulnerability granting root access, is being actively exploited in the wild and has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. A critical authentication bypass in cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940) is under mass exploitation for ransomware deployment. Multiple ransomware groups are actively targeting organizations, including Everest's attack on Canadian financial processor Symcor, and ShinyHunters claiming a breach of Instructure Holdings (Canvas LMS). The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued multiple alerts covering these Linux and cPanel vulnerabilities, plus guidance on social engineering attacks targeting enterprise SaaS environments.

items.description: Linux kernel vulnerability allowing root access exploitation
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued four advisories in the past two weeks covering critical vulnerabilities in Linux (CVE-2026-31431), cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940), and Cisco ASA/FTD devices, plus a warning about social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS environments. The Everest ransomware group has targeted Symcor, a key Canadian financial data processor serving major banks.
canada_landscape.financial: Symcor (Canadian financial data processor serving major banks) targeted by Everest ransomware group
